---
title: "GARCH-X Deposit Volatility by Economic Agent"
categories: [econometrics, financial-stability, garch, deposits]
---

## Problem

Deposit behavior is heterogeneous across economic agents and currency denomination. A single aggregate deposit series can hide important differences in volatility, persistence, and shock transmission.

## Method

A GARCH-X specification can be used to evaluate whether macro-financial variables help explain conditional volatility in deposit changes.

## Implementation sketch

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

set.seed(123)

df <- tibble::tibble(
  date = seq.Date(as.Date("2025-01-01"), by = "day", length.out = 250),
  agent = rep(c("Households", "Private firms"), each = 125),
  dlog_deposits = c(rnorm(125, 0, 0.004), rnorm(125, 0, 0.010))
)

ggplot(df, aes(date, dlog_deposits, color = agent)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ agent, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Synthetic deposit changes by economic agent",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Δ log deposits"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Reproducibility

This public page should use synthetic or anonymized data only. Confidential supervisory data must never be committed to the repository.

## Next steps

- Add model specification.
- Add diagnostics.
- Add robustness checks.
- Add replication instructions.
